mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 8.0 percent signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives equity risk premiums lower, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of industrial production and housing starts indicates synchronized real-economy demand expansion, which improves corporate revenue visibility and compresses equity volatility, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no mean difference between high and low interaction signal states.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate while core inflation remains anchored below 2.5 percent creates a favorable earnings growth environment that lifts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of periods where both series satisfy the condition, or the cross-sectional predictive power vanishes over a 4-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained business hiring cycles, which directly supports aggregate demand and corporate cash flow projections, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return distribution shows no statistically significant positive skew relative to the unconditional mean, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate above 0.25 percent while consumer price inflation simultaneously rises signals tightening monetary policy that increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year walk-forward test, or the predictive Rank IC remains above -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of real consumer spending and employment growth indicates a self-reinforcing demand shock that improves corporate profit margins and reduces equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal's predictive power decays to zero when controlling for market volatility, or forward returns show no significant difference between high and low signal deciles over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production growth outpaces consumer price inflation indicates improving supply chain efficiency and margin expansion, which directly boosts equity earnings per share and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of periods where the divergence condition holds, or the cross-sectional Rank IC drops below 0.01 over any 2-year out-of-sample test.
